oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Curating Library Exhibits for Learning: The "Women in the Botanical Arts" Exhibit as a Model for Academic Library Pedagogy
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Sussmann, Madison
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: This paper describes the development of “Women in the Botanical Arts,” an exhibit created for the [institution] to show how academic libraries can use exhibits as experiential learning tools. Drawing on circulating materials, artists’ books, and Special Collections and Archives, the exhibit highlighted women’s contributions to botanical illustration while encouraging close observation and self-directed engagement. The paper focuses on practical curatorial methods including concept and narrative development, object selection, visual design, label writing, and digital extension, and connects these strategies to principles of experiential learning and learning-centered library design. The exhibit demonstrates approachable, replicable practices that academic libraries can use to activate collections, engage visitors, and integrate exhibit-making into teaching and scholarship.
oai_dc:dc.dc:publisher: Association for Information Science and Technology
